decision.summary: Keep imported function/member Promise call-return receivers closed, but relabel target-present imported call-return obligations as settled-value contracts instead of return-domain proof. Imported targets have an import coordinate, not an in-file body, so PromiseLike result-domain proof alone is insufficient to recover `.then`/`.catch` payloads.
policy: An imported call target proves callee identity only.
Imported function/member Promise receiver recovery needs an explicit settled-value or rejection-channel contract, not selector spelling and not return-domain proof alone.
Import-backed member calls such as `client.load().then(...)` remain distinct from direct `Promise.resolve(...).then(...)` and from synchronous payload code.
DirectFunction and DirectMethod labels still use returned-expression domain proof because those same-file bodies can be evaluated by existing fail-closed recovery code.
The next exact imported slice should define a reusable settled-value evidence capability before opening any imported Promise producer.

renamed_missing_evidence.reason: Imported function target identity has no local body from which the value graph can recover a fulfilled or rejected payload.
Imported member target identity has no local body from which the value graph can recover a fulfilled or rejected payload.

corpus_source_pricing.scan_condition: 120-repo JS/TS lexical source scan for .then/.catch/.finally call-return receivers
limitations: The source scan is a pricing signal, not semantic proof for each source occurrence.
This slice changes reporting vocabulary and regression coverage only; it does not admit imported Promise producers.
Provider modules that export immutable values are covered by the import-snapshot track, but callable imported producers still need a separate settled-value contract.

focused_regression_coverage: Internal missing-evidence mapping now reports imported function/member target-present Promise call-return receivers as settled-value contracts.

A source-level recall-loss fixture with imported function, imported binding member, and imported namespace member Promise receivers emits the settled-value labels and does not fall back to missing imported-member callee proof.
A source-level imported namespace member call-return receiver remains distinct from a direct Promise.resolve(...).then(...) form.
The same imported namespace member call-return receiver remains distinct from synchronous payload code.
closed_boundaries_after_slice: imported function Promise call-return receivers without settled-value contract
imported member Promise call-return receivers without settled-value contract
possible thenable assimilation through unsafe Promise.resolve arguments
Promise constructors, `.finally`, aggregate combinators, and broad scheduling equivalence
next_work: Design the minimal settled-value evidence capability for imported Promise producers.
Price which imported binding/member candidates are first-party wrappers around Promise.resolve/Promise.reject versus framework or remote I/O producers.
Keep ordinary imported member recovery closed until import coordinate, settled channel, callback demand/effect, and hard negatives are all explicit.
